message:
feat(python): masked_invalid python wrapper (franken_numpy-lqpc)

Expose numpy.ma.masked_invalid through fnp-python so Python callers
can mask non-finite entries (NaN and ±inf) with SciPy/NumPy-matching
semantics, advancing the franken_numpy-lqpc bead.

crates/fnp-python/src/lib.rs:
- Add #[pyfunction] masked_invalid(a, copy=true) that implements the
  same pipeline NumPy does internally: compute invalid = ~isfinite(a),
  then delegate to numpy.ma.masked_where(invalid, a, copy=copy).
  Using isfinite rather than isnan is required for float parity
  (NumPy's masked_invalid masks ±inf as well as NaN); the copy kwarg
  is forwarded verbatim so callers keep control of aliasing.
- Register the new function in the fnp_python module so it is
  available as fnp_python.masked_invalid without further wiring.

crates/fnp-ufunc/src/lib.rs:
- Whitespace-only cleanup in the `use` import list alongside the
  existing ma_* symbols (reformatting after the prior parity landings
  so the sorted import block wraps cleanly at the column limit).

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- franken_numpy-9ber (minimum_fill_value python parity) moved back
  to open while the wrapper surface is re-evaluated.
- franken_numpy-lqpc updated_at refresh to keep its in_progress
  timestamp current as the masked_invalid wrapper lands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:

         ma_maximum_fill_value, ma_maximum_fill_value_for_dtype, ma_minimum_fill_value,
-        ma_minimum_fill_value_for_dtype, mediate_ufunc_runtime_policy, modf,
-        nextafter, normalize_fixed_signature_keywords, normalize_signature_keywords, pad_empty,
+        ma_minimum_fill_value_for_dtype, mediate_ufunc_runtime_policy, modf, nextafter,
+        normalize_fixed_signature_keywords, normalize_signature_keywords, pad_empty,
         pad_linear_ramp, pad_stat, parse_fixed_signature_string, parse_gufunc_signature,
         plan_binary_dispatch, plan_binary_dispatch_with_registry,
         plan_binary_dispatch_with_signature, poly2cheb, poly2herm, poly2herme, poly2lag, poly2leg,
